content:
force a smile for a stranger
the next moment my face falls
I’m a spider web of lies
every tale I tell is tall
who wouldn’t miss a home they could never return to?

shopping list:
beans beans beans
sprinkles
asparagus
paper plates
black pepper
salt?
cheese
carrots
more beans.
backup beans.
 emergency beans.
 yummy beans.
I watch a woman buy flowers from the train station Walgreens
I’m afraid that good doesn’t exist only less bad
vital
can’t look away from a train wreck
under yellow skies and rising tides
and the fog washes over us

writing, for me, is like bloodletting
squeezing out the tainted cells in pursuit of healing
prescribed burn in progress
clockwork
not even you can erase the bloodstains
bloodshot
owls hooting in harmony
daytime moon

I see an adolescent bird and I remember that life goes on
I look up and see flowers sprouting out of wood
it smells of spring/the scent of spring
the letter H gouged into a tree still remains
on a day that feels like summer
the facade falls off
there’s a metaphor somewhere in sweeping water with a broom

it’s hard to focus on anything but survival when you’ve been living in a burning building
the stairs crumbled years ago but you didn’t have the energy to care
and your lungs kept overflowing with darkness
as you try and choke out a cry for help but it just sounds like a cough
and they scream at you not to jump but they can’t feel the fire on your back
they don’t know you’re one breath away from asphyxiation
how would they?
having a spark just means you’re ready to live
it’s impossible to explain to someone who has never had to be dragged away from a staring contest with death how liberating it is to feel the sun
not thick walls just bouncy ones

TO DO:
-homework
-buy groceries
-text people back
-laundry
-clean dorm
-cry
so real. that snowman is living in a society.

how does a tree survive the winter?
if it isn’t evergreen
losing leaves, losing light
how does it get by
can’t photosynthesize without chlorophyll
can’t grow without glucose
so how does it not die?
if I were that tree
and I lost all my light
I don’t know how I’d survive
I still hesitate when I step on escalators
oxygen mask
how do I let go of the life I never lived?
it’s just another leap of faith
I stare into the headlights and suddenly I am the deer

stop the thinking 
stop the bleeding
I smell the rain before I see it
tomatoes that taste like the sun
haven’t seen the sky in weeks
finally caught up but at what cost

just outside the splash zone
free from the poison clouds
pretending that I’m safe here
one step away from doubt

staring at the wreckage
fallen houses, broken bones
the purple’s all consuming
think I might be all alone
wake me up when things are okay again
the air after it rains

shopping list:
noodles
snackss
beans
soda
yogurt
I am the raindrops illuminated by a cop car’s headlights
tell me how to stop being mad at the world
can’t tell if it’s anxiety or the caffeine
yesterday feels like a week ago
you never know the value of a moment til’ it’s over
not everything lives when you love it hard enough
tomorrow it'll all be real

the bones of a building
a jerking car
you’re sunlight in the bitter rain
as much as I want time to stop it just keeps on going
being with you is like breathing in stale air
need to stop making self deprecation my only personality trait
there’s a certain aesthetic to eating on the floor

I breathe in color like a drug because it is one, it fills my bloodstream and my lungs, filled with particles of light, I am light, I have ascended into a deeper plane of being now that color has seeped into my veins. if you were to pierce my skin right now I’d bleed technicolor blood, my life is a technicolor dream and I am the dream master this time, the world listens to me because color is all that matters and will matter for all time to come.

you can start over anytime you want
you don’t have to wait until the morning comes
just breathe in, and just breathe out
you’ll be okay, but maybe not right now
when you walk alone you can walk as slow as you want to
serotonin addict
ahhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhh
no one talks about the grief of taking up space
it’s okay.
I never asked to be strong
always say the thing

my anger is here to stay
stop telling me to throw it away
my anger believes that I’ve been wronged
so how can you say that I haven’t been?
my anger cares for me, you see
more than you have ever cared to know
so I’ll hold on to my feelings
just a little longer now
I’ll try, living by my own rules for a while
see if I can ever, just smile
without any intention, any pretending
I’ll try, watch them run from my entrance
never really knowing what comes next
I’ll run along the highway
till no thing’s good and done
my flabber has been gasted
fields of dying trees and tumbleweeds
running a marathon with no end
the lord is a brain juice scientist

dead end
detour
default sad

the world is spinning round
faster than the siren sound

just watch as I turn my body into pain
because I know it’ll make great art
someday
shards of glass can shine like diamonds too
the petrichor stings already
(the art of) lingering
thrumming

I think it was worth waiting for this. for brilliant corals access a gray morning sky—for wispy clouds, and fluffy ones too. rimmed with light, cherry blossom pink and tangerine orange, reflected perfectly in the water below. I think it was worth living for this. I watch a single bird silhouetted against the sky flapping its wings to eternity. hands outstretched, I wish I could grasp the stars. and maybe one day I will but for now I’ll settle with my heart. home to a soul, stretched so thin it’s a miracle it hasn’t snapped, a soul so fragile yet tough, I’m glad it has made it to today. I hope it’ll make it till tomorrow.
I’m gonna naturally select this koi
I like burying myself in people. sinking into their softness letting myself drift away.
eating dinner in the dark
limited/distorted vision
the doorknob to my garage doesn’t open the right way
this game of trust is so hard to play
until the structures in the water look like the chemical image for hydrogen in the periodic table book my sister memorized by the time she was in fifth grade

and all the colors fade to gray
I can’t be bothered to stay
the power of the comfort zone
it feels like I’m smelling ammonia all over again.
I narrate my life as I live it. because maybe if it sounds like a story then it’ll hurt less to live through.
maybe living is staying up every night and regretting it the next day but continuing to do it anyway because you have no sense of self preservation

there’s a ticking time bomb in my chest
it’ll blow when I least expect it to

I make metaphors out of nothing
forcing meaning on the world
I can’t stand a world that doesn’t make
sense to my soul

“even if it burns.”
especially if it burns, I say

im quiet because I have too be
because the world gets a little too loud for me
and I need to shut it out
I’m never really awake, am I?
simply sick of being
I don’t want to be perfect I just want to feel whole
exploded into being.
roadkill

I never fix the things that hurt
rather let my house crash and burn than be the one that mends the pain
I think my well of feeling has run dry

I believe that life is just you, me, us, here, now, together with the world.
and if that’s all we have then that’s all we have and we’ll make of it what we will.
and if winter were a person I think I’d like to be their friend
I have no right to be tired
“I wanna be where the people are, murdering fish and all of my brethren” - joiposidhrxdifjl, to the tune of Part of Your World, 9:32 am, 5/16/2022
I want to feel like the night sky

because breathing is an ugly thing
haggard
if my funeral were tonight would you come and sing me a eulogy

I want to be soft enough so when the blades cut me open I bleed mountains of air
I'll take the next train to the edge of nowhere
climb the fruitless trees to pick peaches of thought
scavenge the carcass of [wonder] and then leave it to rot
bearing horns made of courage
a sword made of heart
maybe I won't let this world tear my story apart
twice baked bread
will I ever walk this earth without flames in my eyes

"there's a...bumpies. that's not what it's called. they are bumpy though!"

"man colleges are not gonna love me. list of personal accomplishments:
I balanced three carpet tiles on my foot.
not two, not one...oh shit it's two."

"it can trample my excessively tall...height"

"ho ho ho ho ho. Santa claus. but suffering."

"I don't know why I thought hand sanitizer would moisturize my body, but it does smell like pears so that counts for something. at least it makes me snap *snaps* hehe. yeah. badum crash...what am I doing with my life anymore"

"my kidney is in pain. my child's leg joint is in pain"

"Santa Claus is like the reverse tax collector. he comes to your house and he gives you things. happy Halloween. *bows* oh shit."

"i love chi...chi...chiropractors. humans are percussion instruments. that's why your bones crack"

"punt the child. Imaginary child. say for instance you have a child. a child, born, freshly created. taken, and kicked. I will be charged for my crimes in the higher court, I bid you adieu. *almost falls*"

"man I should become an acTOR. that's French for person...[stuff I didn't catch]... slap them on the face. hire me now."

"the cold heart of space...welcome to earth ASMR. a podcast for aliens wanting to know about humans. on the planet, if it still exits, oh no this took a turn. and not a right one, but a left one... sometimes the rightest turn can be the wrongest turn. so says the global positioning device in my phone...the GPS lady renamed herself from GPS to LSD not sure what that's about."

"they call this a vestibule that's so cool. a ve-si-tu-ble."

moral
parents are wrong sometimes (most of the time) about what’s best for you so you should just be reckless and make stupid decisions for the sake of being young and happy

manatee
lynx
Crow
eagle
seal
bat
weasel/sable
snow leopard
red panda
raccoon
this flaw in our design

petting a cat
winning cards against humanity (emotions, murder, Facebook)
sparkles on the floor
uno in the corner
music in the stairwell
skipping the line
party bus
the sanctuary of the elevator
find me on clouds of ecstasy
keep out of reach of children
mental health more like mental hell

would you kill me if i died here tonight?
march down to the Underworld, to try and put up a fight?
sing a song of love and spring 
hoping that someone is listening
because maybe I'm the songbird
sick of storms and snakes and sky
so given the choice, of sanctuary
I chose, for the peace of mind

because I didn't tell you that I was ripping at the seams
a tapestry left to bleed
my secrets for all to see
as you watch the drops of grief
pool at the foot of a crumbling lighthouse
only it's too foggy to see the light
but even if it were clear
the light's not there

teach me how to stop living in negative days
in sunsets I never saw, words I never said
how do I not regret everything I never did
teach me how to let go of the life I never lived
teach me how to stop finding myself in broken stories and feeling like I've come home
teach me how to realize that even if I'm lonely I'll never be alone
teach me how to stop hating myself for what meager self-preservation I can manage to scrape up from the bottom of this bowl running empty on a will to live
I'll carve out a flower
engrave it into the wood
if it's the last thing I do
I'll force spring to come
but for me, nothing blooms

so can you really blame me
for leaving this all behind?

suddenly
I hear your song of spring
and I promise, I am listening
to your words of hope and life
telling me that it's better alive

so maybe ill test fate
with every step i take
up towards life, towards the light 
hoping, this time, that we'll both make it out alive.
"you know what they say about progress, you add an 'i v e' and you get an insurance company" - jszchkschlca, 10:50am, 2/24/2022

hold my hand so I can watch the stars
let's reclaim what was always ours
emotions aren't weak, they're beautiful
solid methylene blue + dissolved caustic sugar? sugar and base
I can't help but measure my worth with the only yardstick I was given

so let us be ripped into being
each tear reminding us
of all the hope out there
begrudgingly trudging through sludge

here's to late nights
and early mornings
spent awake
thinking, creating
myself away
out of commission
"the sheer amount of cocaine in my system makes my blood a powerful narcotic" - jxofhlhflhapusp, 12:08pm, 1/28/2022

you were a little rough around the edges

but maybe the string snapped and the thread frayed the rope slipped and fell away
and the train tracks split and the paths diverged and now I'm stranded here in this forest in the dirt

maybe because I haven't heard the phrase "I love you" in too long
maybe our souls, weren't meant to be happy
maybe happy was never ours to have

I'm sorry that we were a vase with too many cracks to hold anything other than our emptiness
I'm sorry

I still hear the doves when I walk past your house
I never met them. You said I would meet them. now I’ll never meet them.

I think I miss

Because that tree in your backyard
Will continue to bloom

and it's so hard to write this poem
because talking to you was like breathing
breathing, I wonder if you're still breathing

and I hate myself for how naive I was
yet still I miss your smile

and weren't we a little too broken to succeed? so why,
why the hell did we believe? because now
I keep looking for you in broken stories
and I wonder if you're still alive

maybe I never think about texting you on the good days because I don't have any anymore
because you
you spoke in metaphor
and maybe that's why this poem is so damn hard to write

and you said that if my raft ever fell apart you'd save me from drowning
you said that if I needed to we both could run away
caring doesn't look good on you
I've never been good at making things beautiful
do you know how many crows can hide in a tree?

precipice
wind in my hair
uneven, rocky surface
patches of dark green moss
loose pebbles and sand
surrounded by bushes and trees
dried up leaves
hearing ocean waves

the weight of gravity pulling me down
a dizzy fear, sickness
chance, anticipation
I know I should get out of bed but I'd rather lie here until my limbs become ash and my lungs are full of moss

looming moon lay silent
its shadow dark with fog
beams dislocate, choke and break
the gutted creature lay
temptation transforms into contempt
the somber shadow flings the remains
over the precipice, only an echo persists

steeling myself in preparation because maybe steel doesn't hurt like we do
and if I'm made out of steel
I won't be able to feel
skyrocket

I am here, am I not?
I am here, is that not enough?
cyanometer

I don't tell you that I am ripping at the seams
a tapestry left to bleed
my imperfections for you to see

would you kill me if I died here tonight?
march down to the underworld and put up a fight?
because the ice melts and so do I
I couldn't even romanticize my life if I wanted to
i never think about texting you on the good days

can you stop time
just for a little while?
why does it hurt me to know that there are so many things I will never know?

when the sunset is beautiful but you can't feel it in your chest
when you know there's things to live for and yet you're still depressed
when you're far beyond exhaustion but you can't bear to rest
what else is there to do but disappear, the world's request?
charon
file not found

fantasies about being okay
that's all they are

"one day I want to go around and play pac man with all the dust motes I can find around my house. it's like eating the snow when it falls except it's probably a carcinogen or something. limited time, because it'll kill you y'know." - josodidujfhf, 3:33pm, 11/24/2021

because suddenly I looked and saw
golden leaves upon the ground
a collapsed perception
why does everyone believe me to be so much more than I am?
